snp3.2:1.1: To me— my mind resolute in exertion
snp3.2:1.3: making a great effort, doing jhāna
snp3.2:1.4: to attain rest from the yoke—
snp3.2:3.2: Only one part is your life.
snp3.2:3.3:
Live, good sir!
Life is better.
snp3.2:3.4: Alive, you can do acts of merit.
snp3.2:4.4: What use is exertion to you?
snp3.2:5.1: Hard to follow —the path of exertion—
snp3.2:5.2: hard to do, hard to sustain.”
snp3.2:5.4: Māra stood in the Awakened One’s presence.
snp3.2:6.1: And to that Māra, speaking thus,
snp3.2:7.2: even the least bit of need.
snp3.2:7.3: Those who have need of merit:
snp3.2:7.4: Those are the ones Māra’s fit to address.
snp3.2:8.1: In me are conviction austerity, persistence,
snp3.2:8.3: Why, when my mind is resolute,
snp3.2:8.4: do you petition me to live?
snp3.2:9.3: Why, when my mind is resolute,
snp3.2:11.3: the mind has no interest in sensuality.
snp3.2:12.1: Sensual passions are your first army.
snp3.2:15.4: but one having defeated it gains bliss.
snp3.2:16.3: Death in battle would be better for me
snp3.2:17.4: by which those with good practices go.
snp3.2:18.1: Seeing the bannered force on all sides—
snp3.2:18.2: the troops, Māra along with his mount—
snp3.2:18.4: May they not budge me from my spot.
snp3.2:19.2: that the world with its devas can’t overcome,
snp3.2:19.4: as an unfired pot with a stone.
snp3.2:20.3: I will go about, from kingdom to kingdom,
snp3.2:21.4:
where, having gone, there’s no grief.”
Māra:
snp3.2:24.1: but not getting anything delicious there,
snp3.2:24.3: Like the crow attacking the rock,
snp3.2:25.1: As he was overcome with sorrow,
snp3.2:25.2: his lute fell from under his arm.
